full_prompt: Design one proprotor hub arm of a three-blade tiltrotor hub for a hybrid-electric tiltrotor benchmark, derived from the XV-15 baseline aerodynamics. Rotor diameter 7.62 m (25 ft), tip speed 237 m/s (778 ft/s) in helicopter mode and 190 m/s in airplane cruise mode. Aircraft DGW 6577 kg (14500 lb); single-rotor thrust at hover OGE with sea-level ISA +15 C = 35600 N per rotor; OEI hover ceiling 1220 m. Hub arm carries one blade root via a pitch-bearing retention with inboard coning-flap hinge 100 mm from hub centerline. Material: Ti-6Al-4V forged (Young's modulus 113.8 GPa, Poisson 0.342, yield 860 MPa, ultimate 950 MPa, density 4430 kg/m^3). Arm outer envelope 450 mm radial length x 120 mm wide x 80 mm thick with a central pitch bearing bore of 60 mm and an outboard blade-retention pin bore of 40 mm. Load cases: (LC1) hover thrust: 11,900 N axial (35,600 / 3 blades) steady plus 3x centrifugal force 150,000 N per blade from the 237 m/s tip speed (blade mass 85 kg at 2.4 m effective radius), applied at the outer pin bore; (LC2) conversion corridor pitch authority: cyclic control moment of 8000 N-m about the pitch axis applied over 2 seconds; (LC3) airplane-mode cruise: 7000 N axial + 50,000 N centrifugal at 190 m/s tip speed; (LC4) OEI hover at 1220 m: steady 12,500 N axial thrust at 95% of centrifugal; (LC5) gust/retreating-blade stall: +/- 4000 N alternating lift on pin bore at 4 Hz (1P) over 1x10^7 cycles for life-cycle fatigue per MIL-HDBK-516C. Pass/fail: (R1) max von Mises across LC1-LC4 <= 633 MPa (ultimate 950 / 1.5); (R2) at yield equivalent, <= 748 MPa; (R3) maximum radial growth at blade-retention pin bore under LC1 centrifugal below 0.2 mm; (R4) fatigue endurance: 10^7 cycle alternating stress at pitch bearing bore below 120 MPa (Ti-6Al-4V notched Kt=3); (R5) modal first in-plane bending of arm above 30 Hz to stay above 1P and below 3P resonance windows. Deliverables: STEP, FEA per case, fatigue, and modal report.
short_prompt:
Design one proprotor hub arm of a three-blade tiltrotor for a hybrid-electric aircraft with XV-15 baseline aerodynamics, rotor diameter 7.62 m, DGW 6577 kg, single-rotor hover thrust 35,600 N, helicopter-mode tip speed 237 m/s and airplane-cruise tip speed 190 m/s. Material class: Ti-6Al-4V forging. Qualify per MIL-HDBK-516C with ultimate FoS 1.5 and yield FoS 1.15; fatigue endurance at 1e7 cycles taken as a Ti-6Al-4V notched (Kt=3) allowable.

Envelope: arm on the order of 450 mm radial x 120 mm wide x 80 mm thick with 60 mm pitch-bearing bore and 40 mm blade-retention pin bore.

Load cases.
- LC1 hover thrust: 11,900 N axial + 150,000 N centrifugal at the outer pin bore.
- LC2 conversion pitch authority: 8000 N-m cyclic moment about the pitch axis over 2 s.
- LC3 airplane cruise: 7000 N axial + 50,000 N centrifugal.
- LC4 OEI hover at 1220 m: 12,500 N axial + 95% centrifugal.
- LC5 fatigue: +/- 4000 N alternating on the pin bore at 4 Hz (1P) for 1e7 cycles.

prompt.title: Tiltrotor proprotor hub arm
prompt.brief: Ti-6Al-4V hub arm for an XV-15-baseline 3-blade tiltrotor, DGW 6577 kg.
